---
title: Factors Shaping Support for Israel Among Democratic Members of the House of
  Representatives
author: "Artur Bakharev"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    df_print: paged
  html_notebook:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    theme: flatly
    highlight: tango
    code_folding: show
---

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(modelsummary)
library(ggplot2)
```

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# DATA LOADING AND PREPARATION
# ==============================================================================
df <- read_csv("data.csv")

df_dem <- df %>%
  filter(Party == "Dem") %>%
  mutate(
    Race = relevel(as.factor(Race), ref = "White"),
    Gender = as.factor(Gender),
    
    # Calculate Protest Surges
    Surge_Israel_V2 = pmax(0, Region_Israel_V2 - Region_Israel_V1),
    Surge_Palestine_V2 = pmax(0, Region_Palestine_V2 - Region_Palestine_V1),
    Surge_Isr_V2_log = log1p(Surge_Israel_V2),
    Surge_Pal_V2_log = log1p(Surge_Palestine_V2),
    
    Surge_Israel_V3 = pmax(0, Region_Israel_V3 - Region_Israel_V2),
    Surge_Palestine_V3 = pmax(0, Region_Palestine_V3 - Region_Palestine_V2),
    Surge_Isr_V3_log = log1p(Surge_Israel_V3),
    Surge_Pal_V3_log = log1p(Surge_Palestine_V3)
  )
```

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# TABLE 1: DYNAMIC SENTIMENT MODELS
# ==============================================================================
# Transition 1 & 2 for Twitter Stance
mod_stance_v2 <- lm(
  Stance_UpTo_V2 ~ Stance_UpTo_V1 + Gender + Race + PVI + JewishPop + AIPAC_norm + Surge_Isr_V2_log + Surge_Pal_V2_log, 
  data = df_dem
)

mod_stance_v3 <- lm(
  Stance_UpTo_V3 ~ Stance_UpTo_V2 + Gender + Race + PVI + JewishPop + AIPAC_norm + Surge_Isr_V3_log + Surge_Pal_V3_log, 
  data = df_dem
)

coef_map_stance <- c(
  "(Intercept)" = "Intercept",
  "Stance_UpTo_V1" = "Previous Stance (Up to V1)",
  "Stance_UpTo_V2" = "Previous Stance (Up to V2)",
  "GenderM" = "Male (vs Female)",        
  "GenderMale" = "Male (vs Female)", 
  "RaceBlack" = "Black (vs White)",
  "RaceHispanic" = "Hispanic (vs White)",
  "RaceAsian" = "Asian (vs White)",
  "RaceJewish" = "Jewish (vs White)",
  "PVI" = "PVI (Cook Index)",
  "JewishPop" = "District Jewish Pop %",
  "AIPAC_norm" = "AIPAC Contributions (Std)",
  "Surge_Isr_V2_log" = "New Pro-Israel Protests (V2, Log)",
  "Surge_Isr_V3_log" = "New Pro-Israel Protests (V3, Log)",
  "Surge_Pal_V2_log" = "New Pro-Palestine Protests (V2, Log)",
  "Surge_Pal_V3_log" = "New Pro-Palestine Protests (V3, Log)"
)

modelsummary(
  list("Stance V2 (Base: V1)" = mod_stance_v2, 
       "Stance V3 (Base: V2)" = mod_stance_v3),
  coef_map = coef_map_stance,
  stars = c('*' = .05, '**' = .01, '***' = .001),
  gof_map = c("nobs", "r.squared", "adj.r.squared", "rmse"),
  title = "Table 1"
)
```

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# TABLE 2
# ==============================================================================
df_dem_avg <- df_dem %>%
  filter(!is.na(Average_Stance_Israel))

mod_stance_overall <- lm(
  Average_Stance_Israel ~ Gender + Race + PVI + JewishPop + AIPAC_norm, 
  data = df_dem_avg
)

# ==============================================================================
# OUTPUT GENERATION
# ==============================================================================
coef_map_stance_overall <- c(
  "(Intercept)" = "Intercept",
  "GenderM" = "Male (vs Female)", 
  "GenderMale" = "Male (vs Female)", 
  "RaceBlack" = "Black (vs White)",
  "RaceHispanic" = "Hispanic (vs White)",
  "RaceAsian" = "Asian (vs White)",
  "RaceJewish" = "Jewish (vs White)",
  "PVI" = "PVI (Cook Index)",
  "JewishPop" = "District Jewish Pop %",
  "AIPAC_norm" = "AIPAC Contributions (Std)"
)
modelsummary(
  list("Stance Score" = mod_stance_overall),
  coef_map = coef_map_stance_overall,
  stars = c('*' = .05, '**' = .01, '***' = .001),
  gof_map = c("nobs", "r.squared", "adj.r.squared", "rmse")
)
```

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# VOTE TRANSITION TABLE
# ==============================================================================
print("Vote Transition: November (V1) to February (V2):")
table(Previous = df_dem$V1, Current = df_dem$V2)

print("Vote Transition: February (V2) to April (V3):")
table(Previous = df_dem$V2, Current = df_dem$V3)

# ==============================================================================
# FIRTH REGRESSIONS
# ==============================================================================
library(logistf)
library(modelsummary)

# Transition 1: November (V1) -> February (V2)
mod_firth_v2 <- logistf(
  V2 ~ V1 + Gender + Race + PVI + JewishPop + AIPAC_norm + Surge_Isr_V2_log + Surge_Pal_V2_log, 
  data = df_dem
)

# Transition 2: February (V2) -> April (V3)
mod_firth_v3 <- logistf(
  V3 ~ V2 + Gender + Race + PVI + JewishPop + AIPAC_norm + Surge_Isr_V3_log + Surge_Pal_V3_log, 
  data = df_dem
)

# ==============================================================================
# TABLE OUTPUT
# ==============================================================================
coef_map_firth <- c(
  "V1" = "Previous Vote (V1)",
  "V2" = "Previous Vote (V2)",
  
  # Identity Controls
  "GenderMale" = "Male (vs Female)",
  "RaceArab" = "Arab (vs White)",
  "RaceAsian" = "Asian (vs White)",
  "RaceBlack" = "Black (vs White)",
  "RaceHispanic" = "Hispanic (vs White)",
  "RaceJewish" = "Jewish (vs White)",
  "RaceNative American" = "Native American (vs White)",
  
  # Structural Controls
  "PVI" = "PVI (Cook Index)",
  "JewishPop" = "District Jewish Pop %",
  "AIPAC_norm" = "AIPAC Contributions (Std)",
  
  # Protest Surges
  "Surge_Isr_V2_log" = "New Pro-Israel Protests (V2, Log)",
  "Surge_Isr_V3_log" = "New Pro-Israel Protests (V3, Log)",
  "Surge_Pal_V2_log" = "New Pro-Palestine Protests (V2, Log)",
  "Surge_Pal_V3_log" = "New Pro-Palestine Protests (V3, Log)"
)

# Combine both models into a single table
modelsummary(
  list("Firth V2 (Base: V1)" = mod_firth_v2, 
       "Firth V3 (Base: V2)" = mod_firth_v3),
  coef_map = coef_map_firth,
  stars = c('*' = .05, '**' = .01, '***' = .001),
  gof_map = c("nobs", "logLik"), 
  title = "Table 3. Firth Penalized Logistic Regression for Vote Switching"
)
```

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# MEAN COMPARISON AND T-TEST
# ==============================================================================
summary_stats <- df_dem %>%
  filter(!is.na(Average_Stance_Israel) & !is.na(V3)) %>%
  group_by(V3) %>%
  summarise(
    Count = n(),
    Mean_Stance = round(mean(Average_Stance_Israel), 3),
    Median_Stance = round(median(Average_Stance_Israel), 3)
  )

# Perform a t-test to check if the difference is statistically significant
t_test_result <- t.test(Average_Stance_Israel ~ V3, data = df_dem)
print(t_test_result)

# ==============================================================================
# VISUALIZATION
# ==============================================================================
ggplot(df_dem %>% filter(!is.na(Average_Stance_Israel) & !is.na(V3)), 
       aes(x = factor(V3, labels = c("Voted 'Nay' (Opposed)", "Voted 'Yea' (Supported)")), 
           y = Average_Stance_Israel)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "gray90", color = "black", outlier.shape = NA, width = 0.5) +
  geom_jitter(color = "gray40", alpha = 0.6, width = 0.15, size = 1.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Figure 3. Distribution of digital stance scores by final roll-call vote on H.R. 8034",
    x = "Final Vote on Substantive Aid Package (H.R. 8034)",
    y = "Average Twitter Stance (-1 = Critical, 1 = Pro-Israel)"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    text = element_text(family = "Arial", size = 11),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5)
  )
```
